items.title: Java Properties
items.summary: The Java Properties class is a simple key,value store, like a Hashtable. The Java Properties class can write properties to disk and read them again, in both property file format and XML format.

items.title: Stream Ops Introduction
items.summary: We have begun the documentation of Stream Ops - the embeddable data streaming engine for Java. Stream Ops enable you to write data records into a stream which is stored on disk, and read the records in the same order again later.

items.title: Compare and Swap in Java
items.summary: Compare and swap is an essential operation in concurrency libraries. This tutorial explains the idea behind compare and swap, and how Java provides compare and swap functionality.

items.title: JDBC Batch Update
items.summary: JDBC batch updates can batch multiple SQL statements into a batch which is sent to the database in a single batch, speeding up the update execution. The JDBC batch update tutorial has just been updated.

items.title: JavaFX SplitPane
items.summary: JavaFX SplitPane is a container component which splits its screen space between two or more controls. Between each two controls is a divider which can be moved by the user to specify how much space each control should take up within the SplitPane.

items.title: JavaFX TabPane
items.summary: JavaFX TabPane is a container component which can contain multiple sections (tabs) which can be displayed by clicking on the tab above the section area.

items.title: Parsing and Formatting Dates in Java
items.summary: Parsing and formatting dates in Java can be done using different classes. This updated tutorial gives a short overview of the options Java provides for parsing and formatting dates.

items.title: Java switch Expression
items.summary: Java 12 added the experimental Java switch expression which enables switch statements to return a value. I have expanded the Java switch tutorial to explain the Java switch expression.

items.title: Mem Ops ObjectPool
items.summary: The Mem Ops ObjectPool component is an object pool which allows you to obtain objects which can be freed back to the object pool for later reuse.

items.title: JavaFX Slider
items.summary: The JavaFX Slider control is enables the user to select a value within a preset interval by sliding a handle from left to right.

items.title: Java Ternary Operator
items.summary: The Java Ternary Operator provides a simplified if-statement construct. The Java Ternary Operator tutorial has been updated to show how to chain more than one ternary operator into a single expression.

items.title: JavaFX ProgressBar
items.summary: The JavaFX ProgressBar control is capable of displaying the progress of some task inside a bar.

items.title: Mem Ops BytesAllocatorManualDefrag
items.summary: The Mem Ops BytesAllocatorManualDefrag is a byte block allocator which can allocate smaller blocks of bytes from a bigger byte array. The allocated blocks need to be explicitly freed again to be reallocated. You will need to manually trigger defragmentation of the free blocks when you find it suitable.

items.title: Java Collections Class
items.summary: The Java Collections class contains several useful utility methods for working with Java Collections, like Lists etc.

items.title: JavaFX Hyperlink
items.summary: The JavaFX Hyperlink is a control that displays a text which can be clicked like a button, just like a link in a web page. Your JavaFX application can respond to clicks on a JavaFX Hyperlink, just like on a Button.

items.title: Mem Ops BytesAllocatorAutoDefrag
items.summary: The Mem Ops BytesAllocatorAutoDefrag enables you to allocate smaller sections of a bigger byte array, and free them again. When freed, the BytesAllocatorAutoDefrag automatically defragments the free blocks of the bigger byte array.

items.title: Mem Ops
items.summary: Mem Ops is a small open source toolkit which provides tools for memory management for Java applications that need to allocate and deallocate byte arrays or objects at a rapid pace.

items.title: JavaFX Accordion
items.summary: The JavaFX Accordion is a control which can contain several sections of content, each of which can be expanded and collapsed.

items.title: Java Streams API
items.summary: The Java Streams API tutorial has been updated a bit, including a video version of part of the material now.

items.title: Java Streams API - Video Tutorial
items.summary: I have published a video about basics of the Java Streams API - for those who prefer a screencast over reading text.

items.title: Java SimpleDateFormat
items.summary: Java SimpleDateFormat tutorial updated. The SimpleDateFormat class is used to parse date strings into Date objects, and format Date objects into strings.

items.title: JavaFX TitledPane
items.summary: The JavaFX TitledPane is a container control which displays a title above its content. The TitledPane can be collapsed and expanded to hide and show its content.

items.title: UTF-8
items.summary: UTF-8 is one of the most popular encodings used for Unicode characters. This UTF-8 tutorial explains how to encode and decode Unicode characters (code points) to and from UTF-8.

items.title: Unicode
items.summary: Unicode is an international standard for how to represent characters of many different alphabets as bytes in a computer. This new unicode tutorial explains the basics of unicode.

items.title: Data Streaming Storage Calculations
items.summary: When implementing data streaming it is useful to know how much storage the solution requires. This tutorial explains how to calculate (approximately) how much storage your data streaming solution will use.

items.title: Java Stack
items.summary: I have updated my Java Stack tutorial. A Java Stack is a data structure where you push element on, and pop element off the top of the stack, like a stack of plates.

items.title: Software Elegance
items.summary: Following some feedback from readers I have added a few more sections and an example to my beginning trail on functional software elegance.

items.title: JavaFX TreeTableView
items.summary: The JavaFX TreeTableView enables you to display trees in JavaFX, and for each node in the tree you can have a row of column values displayed next to it.

items.title: JavaFX TreeView
items.summary: The JavaFX TreeView enables you to display trees which can have the nodes expanded and collapsed inside your JavaFX applications.

items.title: Java Collection Interface
items.summary: I have updated my Java Collection interface tutorial. The Java Collection interface is one of the core interfaces in the Java Collections API.

items.title: Software Elegance
items.summary: I have formalized my guiding sentence containing my three rules for achieving software elegance - functionally elegant software - in this latest tutorial touching the subject of usability.

items.title: CSS Tutorial
items.summary: I have updated my introduction to CSS as a starting point for updating several of the topics in the CSS tutorial.

items.title: Java Arrays
items.summary: A Java array is a collection of variables of the same data type which can be access individually, or traversed one by one. As part of my Java tutorial overhaul, I have given the Java Array tutorial an update too.

items.title: New in Java 12
items.summary: Java 12 is out soon, so I have updated the Java tutorial to list the new features in Java 12.

items.title: Java JDBC Connection
items.summary: A JDBC Connection is used to connect to a relational database from a Java application. I have updated the JDBC Connection tutorial to cover more of its core methods.

items.title: Jackson JsonNode
items.summary: The Jackson JsonNode is Jackson's tree model for representing dynamic JSON object graphs in a Java application. This Jackson JsonNode tutorial explains how to get and set fields etc. on a JsonNode.

items.title: JavaFX TableView
items.summary: The JavaFX TableView class is used to display tabular data inside a JavaFX application. In this JavaFX TableView tutorial I explain how to use the TableView.

items.title: JDBC Tutorial
items.summary: I have updated the JDBC tutorial introduction quite a bit. Now it contains a list of popular relational databases, a full JDBC example, and a better explanation of what JDBC is intended for, among other things.

items.title: Joining Data Streams
items.summary: Joining records from different data streams can be very useful, but also complicates your data streaming pipelines. This data stream joining tutorial explores what to look out for when joining data streams.

items.title: Java Annotations
items.summary: The Java Annotations tutorial has been updated, and a video version of the tutorial embedded.

items.title: Jackson ObjectMapper - CBOR and MessagePack
items.summary: The Jackson ObjectMapper can be used to read and write CBOR and MessagePack encoded data too - two compact binary data formats.

items.title: JavaFX DirectoryChooser
items.summary: The JavaFX DirectoryChooser is a dialog which enables the user to choose a directory. Your JavaFX application can then read what directory the user chose.

items.title: Data Streaming Scalability
items.summary: Scaling a data stream up is easy, but once you scale it out, certain distribution effects need to be taken into consideration.

items.title: Java 11 Features
items.summary: I have added a list of the most important Java 11 features to my Java tutorial introduction.

items.title: Java Iterator
items.summary: The Java Iterator tutorial has been updated with information about the ConcurrentModificationException and remove() method.

items.title: Java Try With Resources
items.summary: The Java Try With Resources tutorial has been improved a lot. It explains how to have resources automatically closed after use, when used inside a Java try-with-resources construct.

items.title: Data Streaming Products
items.summary: I have compiled a list of data streaming products, and shortly explained what categories of products they belong to.

items.title: JavaFX Scene
items.summary: The JavaFX Scene object represents the root of the content area of a JavaFX Stage (application window).

items.title: JavaFX Stage
items.summary: The JavaFX Stage object represents an application window in a JavaFX application.

items.title: Java Functional Composition
items.summary: Functional composition in Java enables you to compose new functions by combining existing functions into a single, new function.

items.title: Data Streaming
items.summary: Data Streaming is a data distribution technique where data producers write data records into an ordered data stream from which data consumers can read the records in the same order.

items.title: Java Stream API
items.summary: The Java Stream API provides a functional approach to processing streams of objects - also known as functional stream processing.

items.title: Java ExecutorService
items.summary: The Java ExecutorService can execute tasks concurrently in separate threads. I have added information about how to cancel tasks, plus a few other changes.

items.title: Java Future
items.summary: The Java Future interface represents the result of a task that is executed concurrently, for instance by a Java ExecutorService.

items.title: Java Callable
items.summary: The Java Callable interface represents a task that can be executed concurrently, for instance by a Java ExecutorService.

items.title: JavaFX WebView
items.summary: The JavaFX WebView enables you to embed web pages or web applications inside your JavaFX applications. A handy way to combine web and desktop apps!

items.title: Java Ternary Operator
items.summary: I had completely forgotten the Java ternary operator in my basic Java tutorial! Well - now it is included.

items.title: New in Java 10
items.summary: Did you miss what was new in Java 10? The Java tutorial has been updated to list the most important changes.

items.title: Java List
items.summary: The Java List tutorial has received a major round of improvements! More will come in a near future.

items.title: Java Set
items.summary: The Java Set tutorial has been updated extensively! More to come soon!
